page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4 server family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, running on an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a strong contrast between single-threaded and multi-threaded performance. The instance achieves top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by its Geekbench single-core score of 2292.0 and bzip3 single-threaded decompression speed of 14.76 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive tasks show significant limitations. The Geekbench multi-core score of 2816.0 and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with large-block read and write speeds rated as poor, reaching only 20.28 GB/sec and 14.17 GB/sec respectively.

Given its hardware profile, the c4-highcpu-2 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require high single-threaded processing power but have low memory footprints. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX supports specialized vector and matrix operations. However, the low memory capacity of 4.0 GB and weak multi-core scaling make it unsuitable for memory-heavy databases, large-scale compilation, or complex multi-threaded applications. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and small-scale web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies on a single physical core with hyper-threading. Benchmark results highlight excellent single-core performance, with top-tier Geekbench single-core and bzip3 decompression scores. However, it exhibits weak multi-core performance and poor memory bandwidth, particularly for large-block operations. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which benefit specific vector and matrix calculations. Given its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic text processing, and small-scale web serving, but is poorly suited for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It offers strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 features 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for light workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine delivering strong single-core performance and advanced instruction sets for memory-light, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and bzip3 decompression benchmarks.
Includes advanced hardware features such as Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets.
Constrained by weak multi-core performance and poor large-block memory bandwidth.
No local storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance.
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, text processing, and basic web serving.
